questions.question: Assuming the EFCC intends to challenge the action filed by you above, within what time and what document will it file to do so?
options: Within 7 days by filing affidavit and preliminary objection
Within 5 days by filing a counter affidavit and a written address
Within 42 days by filing memorandum of appearance and statement of defence
Within 8 days by filing a conditional appearance
questions.explanation: The respondent in a fundamental rights enforcement action must file a counter affidavit and a written address within 5 days.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct about the date for hearing of the petition?
options: It has to be fixed within 14 days of filing the application
It has to be fixed within 7 days of filing the application by the applicant
It has to be fixed within 14 days of responding to the application by the respondent
It has to be fixed within 7 days of responding to the application by the respondent.
questions.explanation: Order 4, Rule 1, FREP Rules, 2009.

questions.question: Which of the following is an incorrect position of the law as regards fundamental enforcement procedures in Nigeria?
options: Leave of the relevant court must be obtained to bring an application to enforce fundamental rights
Non compliance with the rules at any stage in the course of the proceedings shall nullify the action
Only a person who has suffered direct breach of his right can bring an application for fundamental rights enforcement
questions.explanation: All three are incorrect statements: leave is NOT required; non-compliance does NOT automatically nullify the action; and persons other than direct victims can bring FREP applications.

questions.scenario: Ayeni Bassey has been a driver to Senator Dambaba for over 9 years. Senator Dambaba has always suspected that Bassey was stealing from him. He set a trap for Bassey to go and repair his new Range Rover Sport 2018 Model. Unknown to Bassey, the vehicle was actually in good condition having undergone a comprehensive service check by Coscharis the previous week while Bassey was not around. Bassey took the Range Rover to his usual mechanic, Iboza who helped him remove and sell the auxiliary gear sensors worth N450,000. This made the gear selector system to start malfunctioning. Bassey then reported to Senator Dambaba that N800,000 was required to repair the gear system. Furious at Bassey's level of dishonesty, Dambaba unleashed two of his Rottweiler dogs on Bassey and they bit him mercilessly. Having being subdued by the dogs, Dambaba locked Bassey in his garage against the plea of neighbour and Bassey's wife, Mrs. Mary has approached you to enforce Bassey's fundamental rights.
questions.question: In which Court will you file the action?
options: The High Court of the FCT
questions.explanation: This is because it is an issue arising out of the cause of employment and the NIC has exclusive jurisdiction in that regard.
questions.law_reference: National Industrial Court - employment-related fundamental rights

questions.scenario: Ayeni Bassey has been a driver to Senator Dambaba for over 9 years. Senator Dambaba locked Bassey in his garage. Bassey's wife, Mrs. Mary has approached you to enforce Bassey's fundamental rights. You have filed the processes.
questions.question: The processes you have filed may be served on Senator Dambaba by any of the following except:
questions.explanation: The Court Registrar is not a process server.

questions.scenario: Ayeni Bassey has been a driver to Senator Dambaba for over 9 years. Senator Dambaba locked Bassey in his garage. Bassey's wife, Mrs. Mary has approached you to enforce Bassey's fundamental rights. The Court is hearing the application.
questions.question: The orders the Court can make upon hearing the application does not include:
questions.explanation: Mandamus is a judicial review mandating a public officer to perform his duties. It is not an order granted in an application for fundamental rights enforcement.
